meta_title: For Founders: Claude Code skills setup to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Claude Code skills setup for AI Builders
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn to use Claude Code skills setup and Antigravity to build, iterate, and launch your SaaS MVP faster than ever before.
content.answer_block: To stop abandoning projects, you must reduce the friction between idea and implementation. By setting up Claude Code with Antigravity, you transform your CLI into a autonomous engineering partner. This setup allows you to scaffold, debug, and deploy logic instantly, shifting your focus from syntax errors to market-facing features.
content.intro:
The graveyard of abandoned SaaS projects is littered with brilliant ideas that died because the founder got lost in the weeds of implementation. You start with a vision of a scalable platform, only to find yourself three weeks deep in dependency hell, losing momentum and passion with every failed build. The bottleneck isn't your vision; it's your execution velocity.

It is time to change the architecture of your workflow. By integrating Claude Code with Antigravity, you move from manual coding to 'architectural steering.' You become the conductor of an AI orchestra, turning your natural language requirements into production-grade code, ensuring that your projects move from the whiteboard to the browser at record speed.

steps.title: Initialize the Project Architecture
steps.description: Establish a robust project structure that handles environment variables and dependencies, preventing common 'it works on my machine' errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a production-ready SaaS directory structure including /src, /api, /tests, and a .env.example file. Focus on modularity.

steps.title: Configure Claude Code Context
steps.description: Define the project constraints and technical stack so Claude understands the boundaries of your codebase from the start.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a .claudeignore and a context file that defines our tech stack: Next.js, Supabase, and Tailwind CSS. Emphasize modular components.

steps.title: Implement Core Business Logic
steps.description: Use AI to write the critical path features of your application, ensuring each function is unit-testable and clean.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build an authentication service using Supabase. Include sign-up, sign-in, and password reset flows with proper error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Implement auth service with Supabase"

steps.title: Automated Debugging and Linting
steps.description: Force the AI to perform a self-review of the generated code to identify potential memory leaks or performance bottlenecks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current /src directory for performance bottlenecks and security vulnerabilities. Suggest and apply fixes.

steps.title: Deploy to Staging
steps.description: Finalize the build and push to a live URL, completing the loop from code to user-facing product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment script that validates the build and pushes to Vercel/Netlify. Ensure all environment variables are mapped.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Deploy current build to staging environment"

pro_tips: Always define a 'persona' in your Antigravity prompt; telling the AI to act as a 'Senior SaaS Architect' significantly improves code quality.
Use Claude Code's 'review' command before every commit to ensure your AI-generated code doesn't drift from your original architectural requirements.
Keep your project context file small; update it every time you add a new major feature to prevent the AI from hallucinating outdated dependencies.
Leverage the 'diff' mode in Claude Code to visually inspect changes before applying them, which helps you learn the underlying code structure.
Create a 'custom instruction' file for repetitive boilerplate tasks so you don't have to re-explain your preferred coding patterns.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, focus on gathering user feedback for 48 hours, then return to Claude Code to iterate on the top-requested feature.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the blueprint to stop dreaming and start shipping. The only difference between a founder with an idea and a founder with a business is the consistent application of code. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
